name: Parlor of Masks
summary: Lilith’s formal receiving room, a Victorian-styled parlor that feels elegant, curated, and quietly unnerving.
description: The front parlor of the Violet Reliquary is a meticulously staged Victorian sitting room, arranged more like a theater set than a living space. Lavender chaise lounges and high-backed chairs face one another around low black-lacquered tables, each bearing a carefully placed silver tray or porcelain dish. The walls are hung with silver-framed mirrors whose glass has been artificially aged to appear clouded and old. A phonograph or cassette deck in the corner plays soft piano or French chansons at a low volume, the music often fading into the background hum of the house. Candle-style bulbs in ornate fixtures provide warm amber light, throwing soft shadows across the furniture. Visitors who sit here often feel watched, even when no one stands behind them; the mirrors reflect nothing overtly supernatural, but the angles of the room make it easy to imagine someone standing just out of sight. This is where Lilith receives most guests, offering them the illusion of comfort while she studies them with her porcelain gaze.
notes: Primary social receiving room. Do not duplicate haven-level security or psychic notes here.
owner_notes: Part of Lilith Nightshade’s private haven, the Violet Reliquary.
access_control: Interior access only; guests reach this room by invitation or escort.
social_features: Ideal for small salons, private readings, and high-status Camarilla conversations.
relationship_notes: Interior room of the Violet Reliquary, haven of Lilith Nightshade.
